Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Starting with the activities involved RO CA RI PI QC PO GSL D IC RP Let me list them again to be clear RO Receive Order CA Check Availability RI Reserve Items PI Pick Items QC Quality Check PO Pack Order GSL Generate Shipping Label D Dispatch IC Invoice Customer RP Record Payment

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: In a typical order process receiving an order should happen once per order and maybe CA also once

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Maybe there are no equivalence constraints in this scenario Or perhaps CA and RI Because checking availability and reserving items might both need to happen once per order

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait the description says If the first activity occurs then the second activity should have been executed previously So for CA to occur RO must have occurred before So the pair would be CA RO in always before

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So correcting Always Before X Y X can only occur if Y has already occurred So Y must come before X

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the always before set would contain pairs where the first element is the activity that depends on the second having occurred before This means CA RO RI CA PI RI QC PI PO QC GSL PO D GSL

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Assuming that IC happens after D as invoicing is often done post shipment So IC is generated after dispatch RP when payment is received

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus for IC to occur D must have occurred before IC D in always before For RP to occur IC must have occurred before RP IC in always before

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always After X Y means if X occurs then Y must occur after X So for each consecutive pair in the sequence we can have always after RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D D IC IC RP

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Never Together means they cannot both occur in the same case Given the activities listed it is possible there are no never together pairs So the set is empty

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Assuming each activity happens exactly once per case activ freq for all would be 1

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: What if an order can have multiple items leading to multiple picks The scenario says a new customer order which could have multiple items So Pick Items could occur multiple times

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Given that the scenario does not specify safest to assume each activity occurs exactly once so activ freq equals 1 for all activities

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Directly Follows means if the first activity occurs then the second shall occur immediately after So the directly follows pairs would be RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D D IC IC RP

Name: Hypothesis Generation - NE
Text: For equivalence earlier thought IC RP since both once So include IC RP and possibly others

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Now checking for possible mistakes For equivalence IC RP if IC occurs once RP occurs once so their counts are equal
